best_extraction.title: Spinal Cord Stimulation for the Failed Back Syndrome
best_extraction.study_question: Is spinal cord stimulation effective for failed back syndrome pain?
best_extraction.population_assessed: 32 failed back syndrome patients with post-laminectomy lumbar and lower extremity pain.
best_extraction.outcome_measures: Pain relief, employment status, narcotic use, recommendations, complications.
best_extraction.inclusion_criteria: Failed back syndrome, prior lumbar laminectomy, lumbar or leg pain, no mechanical instability.
best_extraction.answer_to_study_question: SCS improved pain in most patients initially; benefit declined by 5 years, with reduced narcotic use.

reasoning: Anthropic and Google strongly agreed on bibliographic fields, design, sample size, and core findings; OpenRouter provided no usable data. I merged complementary clinical details and kept wording conservative where long-term efficacy percentages differed.
